entities.name: MICRONUTRIENT MALNUTRITION
entities.description: Deficiency of essential micronutrients affecting over two billion people globally

entities.name: ZINC
entities.description: Essential micronutrient whose deficiency is prevalent in humans and cereal grains

entities.name: IRON
entities.description: Essential micronutrient whose deficiency is prevalent in humans and cereal grains

entities.name: IODINE
entities.description: Essential micronutrient whose deficiency is prevalent globally

entities.name: VITAMIN A
entities.description: Essential micronutrient whose deficiency is prevalent globally

entities.name: DEVELOPING COUNTRIES
entities.description: Regions where resource-poor families are most affected by micronutrient malnutrition

entities.name: FOOD CROPS
entities.description: Agricultural plants that can suffer from Fe and Zn deficiency, affecting yield and quality

entities.name: CROP YIELDS
entities.description: The productivity of crops, which is decreased by Fe and Zn deficiency

entities.name: NUTRITIONAL QUALITY
entities.description: The nutrient density of crops, which is decreased by Fe and Zn deficiency

entities.name: FE ZN DEFICIENT SOILS
entities.description: Soils lacking iron and zinc, contributing to deficiencies in crops and humans

entities.name: CEREAL CROPS
entities.description: Staple crops that are inherently low in Fe and Zn concentrations in their grain

entities.name: GRAIN
entities.description: The seed part of cereal crops where micronutrient concentrations are targeted for enhancement

entities.name: ZINC DEFICIENCY HEALTH PROBLEMS
entities.description: Health impairments in humans resulting from zinc-deficient diets

entities.name: PHYSICAL DEVELOPMENT
entities.description: Human growth and physical maturation impaired by zinc deficiency

entities.name: IMMUNE SYSTEM
entities.description: The biological system responsible for immunity, impaired by zinc deficiency

entities.name: BRAIN FUNCTION
entities.description: Cognitive and neurological functions impaired by zinc deficiency

entities.name: GENETIC BIOFORTIFICATION
entities.description: A sustainable and cost-effective approach to increase Fe and Zn concentrations in grain
